title: VxRail vCenter Plugin Availability and VxM API Heartbeat Health
exclusions: Dell EMC PowerStore based dHCI and HPE Alletra dHCI stacks use different management planes and belong in separate use cases, not this VxM HTML5 path. Pure VMware vSAN ReadyNode clusters without VxRail Manager lack this plugin and API surface. VxRail Edge satellite nodes during deliberate disconnection windows should be filtered out of lookups. VxRail Self Deployment Mode clusters that do not expose the standard VxM API contract you modeled are excluded until ingestion matches reality. Pre VxRail seven HTML Flash clients are deprecated and out of scope for this HTML5 and REST contract.
evidence: Saved search uc_19_2_32_vxm_plugin_sso_scheduler on index=vxrail union vmware indexes as deployed with vxrail_vxm_owner.csv version history, thirteen month retention, and example ticket attachment pairing vCenter Client Plugins screenshot to Splunk row.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab index post vmware:vc:event lines that include RegisterExtensionFailedEvent for a cluster_id present in vxrail_vxm_owner along with vxrail:vxm:sso showing seven days remaining and vxrail:vxm:scheduled_jobs with missed equal one; the saved search should return severity critical or high covering plugin and SSO or scheduler combined.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Set change_freeze_until two days ahead in the lookup for a VxM while injecting synthetic warning tier latency; confirm severity_bucket reads info for that row in the alerting copy while a reporting copy without freeze still shows warning for audit.

dataSources: PRIMARY custom HEC poller against VxRail Manager REST at https://vxm-fqdn/rest/vxm with OAuth POST /rest/vxm/v1/oauth/token or basic auth per release, landing in index=vxrail: sourcetype=vxrail:vxm:system from GET /rest/vxm/v1/system plus initialize fields from GET /rest/vxm/v1/system/initialize/status flattened into the same poll; sourcetype=vxrail:vxm:sso from GET /rest/vxm/v1/system/sso fields vsphere_sso_state vsphere_sso_certificate_expiry last_sso_validation; sourcetype=vxrail:vxm:scheduled_jobs from GET /rest/vxm/v1/system/scheduled-jobs with job_name last_run next_run last_status; sourcetype=vxrail:vxm:health from GET /rest/vxm/v1/system/health for overall_health and component rows such as lcm callhome support-assist. SECONDARY Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere Forwarder sourcetype=vmware:vc:event for com.vmware.cis.cm.NewExtensionEvent RegisterExtensionFailedEvent PluginUnpackFailedEvent families; sourcetype=vmware:vc:log for ExtensionManager errors. TERTIARY optional Splunk_TA_nix curl TCP TLS probe to GET https://vxm-fqdn/rest/vxm/v1/system without bearer token producing sourcetype=vxrail:vxm:probe with http_status_code total_time_ms.
app: Custom HEC poller for VxRail Manager (VxM) REST API; Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere Forwarder for vCenter event corroboration; (optional) Splunk_TA_nix for synthetic TLS probe
spl:
`comment("UC-19.2.32 VxRail management plane: vCenter extension failure corroboration, VxM SSO trust horizon, internal scheduled health jobs, TLS API heartbeat p99. SOURCES: vxrail:vxm:system, vxrail:vxm:sso, vxrail:vxm:scheduled_jobs, vxrail:vxm:health, vxrail:vxm:probe, vmware:vc:event, vmware:vc:log. LOOKUP: vxrail_vxm_owner (vxm_fqdn, cluster_id, owner_team, target_min_sso_days_remaining, target_max_api_p99_ms, target_max_missed_jobs_24h, change_freeze_until; optional vcenter_fqdn). OWNER: hci_platform. FREEZE: change_freeze_until demotes routed rows to info.")`
| inputlookup vxrail_vxm_owner
| eval _time=now()
| eval vxm_fqdn=tostring(coalesce(vxm_fqdn, vxmHost, manager_fqdn, ""))
| eval cluster_id=tostring(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, ""))
| eval owner_team=tostring(coalesce(owner_team, ownerTeam, ""))
| eval target_min_sso_days_remaining=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_min_sso_days_remaining, targetMinSsoDaysRemaining, "30")), 10)
| eval target_max_api_p99_ms=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_api_p99_ms, targetMaxApiP99Ms, "5000")), 10)
| eval target_max_missed_jobs_24h=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_missed_jobs_24h, targetMaxMissedJobs24h, "1")), 10)
| eval change_freeze_until=tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, changeFreezeUntil, ""))
| eval vcenter_fqdn=tostring(coalesce(vcenter_fqdn, vcenterFqdn, ""))
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id
    [| search (index=vmware OR index=vxrail OR index=hci) (sourcetype="vmware:vc:event" OR sourcetype="vmware:vc:log") earliest=-1h@h latest=@h
      | eval cid=tostring(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, computeResourceId, compute_resource_id, ""))
      | eval evt=tostring(coalesce(EventTypeId, event_type, type, Type, ""))
      | eval raw_l=tostring(_raw)
      | eval is_ext_fail=if(((like(evt, "%RegisterExtensionFailedEvent%")) OR (like(evt, "%PluginUnpackFailedEvent%")) OR (match(raw_l, "(?i)RegisterExtensionFailed")) OR (match(raw_l, "(?i)PluginUnpackFailed"))), 1, 0)
      | where ((is_ext_fail=1) AND (len(cid)>0))
      | stats count AS plugin_register_cluster BY cid
      | rename cid AS cluster_id ]
| join type=left max=0 vcenter_fqdn
    [| search (index=vmware OR index=vxrail OR index=hci) (sourcetype="vmware:vc:event" OR sourcetype="vmware:vc:log") earliest=-1h@h latest=@h
      | eval vckey=tostring(coalesce(vcenter, vCenter, vcenter_fqdn, host, ""))
      | eval evt=tostring(coalesce(EventTypeId, event_type, type, Type, ""))
      | eval raw_l=tostring(_raw)
      | eval is_ext_fail=if(((like(evt, "%RegisterExtensionFailedEvent%")) OR (like(evt, "%PluginUnpackFailedEvent%")) OR (match(raw_l, "(?i)RegisterExtensionFailed")) OR (match(raw_l, "(?i)PluginUnpackFailed"))), 1, 0)
      | where ((is_ext_fail=1) AND (len(vckey)>0))
      | stats count AS plugin_register_vc BY vckey
      | rename vckey AS vcenter_fqdn ]
| eval plugin_register_failures_1h=max(coalesce(plugin_register_cluster, 0), coalesce(plugin_register_vc, 0))
| join type=left max=0 vxm_fqdn
    [| search index=vxrail sourcetype="vxrail:vxm:sso" earliest=-24h@h latest=@h
      | eval vxm_fqdn=tostring(coalesce(vxm_fqdn, vxmHost, manager_fqdn, ""))
      | eval exp_raw=tostring(coalesce(vsphere_sso_certificate_expiry, vsphereSsoCertificateExpiry, ""))
      | eval exp_epoch=coalesce(strptime(exp_raw, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), strptime(exp_raw, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z"), strptime(exp_raw, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"), strptime(exp_raw, "%Y-%m-%d"), 0)
      | eval sso_days_remaining=if((exp_epoch>0), ceil((exp_epoch - now()) / 86400), null())
      | eval sso_trust_state=tostring(coalesce(vsphere_sso_state, vsphereSsoState, ""))
      | stats latest(sso_days_remaining) AS sso_days_remaining latest(sso_trust_state) AS sso_trust_state max(_time) AS sso_poll_time BY vxm_fqdn ]
| join type=left max=0 vxm_fqdn
    [| search index=vxrail sourcetype="vxrail:vxm:scheduled_jobs" earliest=-24h@h latest=@h
      | eval vxm_fqdn=tostring(coalesce(vxm_fqdn, vxmHost, manager_fqdn, ""))
      | eval last_run_epoch=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(last_run_epoch, lastRunEpoch, "0")), 10)
      | eval next_run_epoch=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(next_run_epoch, nextRunEpoch, "0")), 10)
      | eval last_status=tostring(coalesce(last_status, lastStatus, ""))
      | eval now_wall=now()
      | eval grace=900
      | eval missed=if((((next_run_epoch>0) AND (now_wall>(next_run_epoch+grace))) AND ((last_run_epoch<next_run_epoch) OR match(last_status, "(?i)fail|error|timeout"))), 1, 0)
      | stats sum(missed) AS missed_jobs_24h BY vxm_fqdn ]
| join type=left max=0 vxm_fqdn
    [| search index=vxrail (sourcetype="vxrail:vxm:probe" OR sourcetype="vxrail:vxm:system") earliest=-24h@h latest=@h
      | eval vxm_fqdn=tostring(coalesce(vxm_fqdn, vxmHost, manager_fqdn, ""))
      | eval latency_ms=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(http_latency_ms, httpLatencyMs, total_time_ms, curl_time_ms, poll_latency_ms, "0")), 10)
      | where ((latency_ms>0) AND (len(vxm_fqdn)>0))
      | bin _time span=1h
      | stats perc99(latency_ms) AS hourly_p99 BY vxm_fqdn, _time
      | sort 0 + vxm_fqdn + _time
      | streamstats current=f window=24 last(hourly_p99) AS p99_now first(hourly_p99) AS p99_start BY vxm_fqdn
      | eval api_p99_trend_delta=round(p99_now - p99_start, 2)
      | stats latest(hourly_p99) AS api_p99_latency_ms latest(api_p99_trend_delta) AS api_p99_trend_delta BY vxm_fqdn ]
| join type=left max=0 vxm_fqdn
    [| search index=vxrail sourcetype="vxrail:vxm:health" earliest=-2h@h latest=@h
      | eval vxm_fqdn=tostring(coalesce(vxm_fqdn, vxmHost, manager_fqdn, ""))
      | eval vxm_health_state=tostring(coalesce(overall_health, overallHealth, health, ""))
      | stats latest(vxm_health_state) AS vxm_health_state BY vxm_fqdn ]
| eval api_p99_latency_ms=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(api_p99_latency_ms, "0")), 10)
| eventstats median(sso_days_remaining) AS org_median_sso_days BY owner_team
| eventstats min(sso_days_remaining) AS org_min_sso_fleet
| eval sso_delta_vs_org=if((isnotnull(sso_days_remaining) AND isnotnull(org_median_sso_days)), round(sso_days_remaining - org_median_sso_days, 1), null())
| eval sso_vs_fleet_min=if((isnotnull(sso_days_remaining) AND isnotnull(org_min_sso_fleet)), round(sso_days_remaining - org_min_sso_fleet, 1), null())
| eval cft1=strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z")
| eval cft2=strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| eval cft3=strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")
| eval cft4=strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%d")
| eval freeze_active=if((len(trim(change_freeze_until))>0) AND (now()<coalesce(cft1, cft2, cft3, cft4, 0)), 1, 0)
| eval sev_raw=case(
    ((plugin_register_failures_1h>0)), "critical",
    ((isnotnull(sso_days_remaining)) AND ((sso_days_remaining<=7) OR (sso_days_remaining<1) OR match(sso_trust_state, "(?i)invalid|fail|error|expired"))), "critical",
    ((missed_jobs_24h>target_max_missed_jobs_24h)), "high",
    ((isnotnull(sso_days_remaining)) AND (sso_days_remaining<=target_min_sso_days_remaining)), "high",
    (((api_p99_latency_ms+0)>target_max_api_p99_ms) OR ((api_p99_trend_delta+0)>1000)), "warning",
    ((match(vxm_health_state, "(?i)warning|error|degraded")) OR ((isnotnull(api_p99_trend_delta)) AND ((api_p99_trend_delta+0)>500))), "warning",
    1=1, "info" )
| eval severity_bucket=if((freeze_active=1), "info", sev_raw)
| where severity_bucket!="info"
| table vxm_fqdn, cluster_id, owner_team, severity_bucket, sev_raw, freeze_active, plugin_register_failures_1h, sso_days_remaining, sso_trust_state, org_median_sso_days, org_min_sso_fleet, sso_delta_vs_org, sso_vs_fleet_min, missed_jobs_24h, api_p99_latency_ms, api_p99_trend_delta, vxm_health_state, target_max_api_p99_ms, target_min_sso_days_remaining, target_max_missed_jobs_24h
description: Correlates vCenter HTML5 extension registration failures from vmware:vc:event and vmware:vc:log with VxRail Manager REST signals for SSO trust, scheduled internal health work, component health, and TLS or tokenized API latency, so operations detect a dead VxRail plugin or broken SSO before administrators open an LCM workflow, detect scheduler stalls before the next upgrade window, and separate management plane outages from data plane cluster summaries covered elsewhere in the catalogue.
value: Platform teams gain ticket ready evidence that the control path between vCenter and VxRail Manager matches what humans experience in the client UI, including early certificate horizon, missed health jobs, and slow or drifting API response percentiles, which prevents surprise bridge calls during change windows and keeps compliance scans from silently stalling when trust expires.
implementation: Stand up index=vxrail HEC with five minute and fifteen minute REST polls, forward vmware:vc:event and vmware:vc:log for extension manager, optionally add Splunk_TA_nix curl probe, load lookups/vxrail_vxm_owner.csv, save the spl as uc_19_2_32_vxm_plugin_sso_scheduler with cron */15 * * * * and a parallel non-freeze dashboard search.
visualization: Single values for critical VxM count and minimum SSO days remaining; table of overdue scheduled jobs; timechart of API p99 latency and drift; highlight panel listing plugin_register_failures_1h alongside sso_trust_state; raw drill to vmware:vc:log for ExtensionManager stack lines.

references.title: Dell VxRail administration and API documentation
Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere Forwarder (Splunkbase 5380)
Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux Splunk_TA_nix (Splunkbase 833)

knownFalsePositives: Planned VxRail Manager upgrade replays extension registration and can raise transient vmware:vc:event counts until the platform stabilizes. Planned vCenter upgrade or patching removes and reinstalls client extensions with similar short lived bursts. Supervised Tomcat restart for log maintenance yields brief REST gaps that clear on the next poll. Quarterly SSO password rotation for the mystic style service account changes trust until operators update VxRail; treat as expected when the rotation ticket is open. Enterprise root or issuing CA renewal for vCenter raises new trust chain fingerprints without implying outage. HCI Mesh discovery sweeps may spike lcm component warnings for minutes during inventory growth.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites

You need index=vxrail for all customer HEC lines that wrap VxRail Manager REST, plus an index such as index=vmware or index=hci for Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere Forwarder streams. The sourcetype contract you implement in props.conf is vxrail:vxm:system from GET https://vxm-fqdn/rest/vxm/v1/system with flattened fields version, health, mystic_node_state and poll metadata http_status and http_latency_ms when you time each call; include initialize status from GET /rest/vxm/v1/system/initialize/status on the same sourcetype family or duplicate with an initialize_state field so plugin registration text is visible next to version; vxrail:vxm:sso from GET /rest/vxm/v1/system/sso carrying vsphere_sso_state, vsphere_sso_certificate_expiry, last_sso_validation in ISO or epoch as emitted by your normalizer; vxrail:vxm:scheduled_jobs from GET /rest/vxm/v1/system/scheduled-jobs with one HEC event per poll containing each job row flattened as job_name, last_run, next_run, last_status; vxrail:vxm:health from GET /rest/vxm/v1/system/health with component rows collapsed into overall_health plus optional lcm component health strings; optional vxrail:vxm:probe from Splunk_TA_nix scripted curl that records tls_ok, http_status_code, and total_time_ms without using the OAuth token so TLS outages still page when token rotation is the failure mode only.

The Splunk Add-on for VMware vSphere Forwarder must expose vmware:vc:event and vmware:vc:log from the same vCenter that hosts the VxRail HTML5 extension. Filter in SPL for com.vmware.cis.cm.RegisterExtensionFailedEvent, PluginUnpackFailedEvent, NewExtensionEvent when paired with failure text, and ExtensionManager log lines that mention unpack or registration failure even when VxRail Manager REST still returns Healthy on its own API.

Service account on VxRail Manager: create or reuse a read-only mystic or svtcli style account with mystic_collector_role or the documented read-only API role for your train. The poller obtains a bearer token via POST /rest/vxm/v1/oauth/token when OAuth is required, or uses basic authentication when your release still allows it. Never store the password in Git; use a vault or OS secret and a systemd EnvironmentFile on the collection host.

Lookup file lookups/vxrail_vxm_owner.csv deployed to Search Heads. transforms.conf stanza on the Search Head app that owns governance:

[vxrail_vxm_owner]
filename = vxrail_vxm_owner.csv
case_sensitive_match = 0
min_matches = 0 Required header columns exactly: vxm_fqdn, cluster_id, owner_team, target_min_sso_days_remaining, target_max_api_p99_ms, target_max_missed_jobs_24h, change_freeze_until. Optional but strongly recommended eighth column vcenter_fqdn populated with the FQDN operators type into the vSphere Client so vmware:vc:event rows that lack cluster_id still join through the vcenter join arm in the canonical SPL.

SSO governance band: warn when certificate or computed days to expiry falls at or below the lookup target_min_sso_days_remaining default thirty days in the alert ladder before critical at seven days or expired, or when vsphere_sso_state shows invalid, failed, or expired. Synthetic TLS probe rationale: the management API can return four zero one when only application authentication breaks while TCP, TLS, and an unauthenticated GET /rest/vxm/v1/system from a monitoring host still proves the listener and certificate chain are alive; run curl with --max-time eight to twelve seconds and index the JSON line to vxrail:vxm:probe.

HEC token name example vxrmgmt_hec_prod in local inputs.conf on the Heavy Forwarder receiving poller posts, indexes equal vxrail, acknowledgement enabled on the indexer tier, connectivity limited to management networks that already reach VxRail Manager virtual IP and vCenter.

NTP must align among poller host, vCenter, VxRail Manager virtual machine, and Splunk indexers so scheduled job overdue logic that compares last_run and next_run does not fire during harmless clock skew. Position this practice after UC-19.2.13 is operating so cluster_id labels on the lookup match the cluster inventory used in sibling searches, even though UC-19.2.13 reads data plane summaries while this use case reads management plane posture.

Step 1 — Configure data collection

local inputs.conf excerpt for the dedicated HEC acknowledgement channel:

[http://vxrmgmt_hec_prod]
disabled = 0
indexes = vxrail
index = vxrail
sourcetype = vxrail:vxm:system
token = REDACTED
ackEnabled = true

Many deployments pass sourcetype per event in the JSON wrapper instead of relying on the token default; both patterns work if props stanzas exist for every name.

props.conf on the first parsing tier for each sourcetype family:

[vxrail:vxm:system]
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
LINE_BREAKER = ([\r\n]+)
TRUNCATE = 2000000
INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json

Repeat SHOULD_LINEMERGE false, LINE_BREAKER, TRUNCATE two million, JSON extraction for vxrail:vxm:sso, vxrail:vxm:scheduled_jobs, vxrail:vxm:health, and vxrail:vxm:probe. If you embed poll_utc in ISO form, map TIME_PREFIX or rely on arrival time consistently.

Python three dot ten pseudocode for the polling loop using requests, with ten second connect and forty five second read timeouts, certificate verify pointed at your internal CA bundle, and exponential backoff on four twenty nine responses:

```python
import time, requests

def token(session, base, user, pwd):
    r = session.post(f"{base}/rest/vxm/v1/oauth/token",
                     json={"grant_type":"password","username":user,"password":pwd}, timeout=30)
    r.raise_for_status()
    return r.json()["access_token"]

def poll_pair(session, base, path, hdr, label):
    t0 = time.time()
    r = session.get(base + path, headers=hdr, timeout=45, verify=CA_BUNDLE)
    ms = int((time.time() - t0) * 1000)
    try:
        body = r.json()
    except Exception:
        body = {"raw": r.text[:8000]}
    return {"path": path, "label": label, "http_status": r.status_code, "http_latency_ms": ms, "body": body}

while True:
    tok = {"Authorization": "Bearer " + token(sess, BASE, USER, PASS)}
    for path, label, st in [
        ("/rest/vxm/v1/system", "system", "vxrail:vxm:system"),
        ("/rest/vxm/v1/system/sso", "sso", "vxrail:vxm:sso"),
        ("/rest/vxm/v1/system/initialize/status", "init", "vxrail:vxm:system"),
        ("/rest/vxm/v1/system/scheduled-jobs", "jobs", "vxrail:vxm:scheduled_jobs"),
        ("/rest/vxm/v1/system/health", "health", "vxrail:vxm:health"),
    ]:
        row = poll_pair(sess, BASE, path, tok, label)
        row["vxm_fqdn"] = VXM_FQDN
        row["cluster_id"] = CLUSTER_UUID
        hec_send({"event": row, "sourcetype": st, "time": int(time.time())})
    sleep_until_next_schedule()
```

PowerShell callers can use Invoke-RestMethod with SkipCertificateCheck only in lab; production should pin CA trust.

cron schedule: five minutes for system and health pulls, fifteen minutes for sso and scheduled_jobs, ninety second jitter. Forwarder inputs for VMware follow Splunkbase guidance; do not strip low level Extension Manager events at props if this UC must see registration failure narratives.

Splunk_TA_nix optional inputs.conf scripted curl example writes one JSON line with total_time_ms, http_status_code, vxm_fqdn, and sourcetype vxrail:vxm:probe every five minutes.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Paste the spl field from this JSON into Search exactly. Save as uc_19_2_32_vxm_plugin_sso_scheduler. In the Splunk save alert dialog set the time range to earliest equals minus twenty four hours at the hour and latest equals now so ad hoc reruns match the join windows baked into each subsearch. Alert on cron star slash fifteen star star star star with per result or number of results greater than zero depending on whether you keep the final where clause that filters info only. Suppress ninety minutes on concatenation of vxm_fqdn and severity for warning and high, allow immediate repeat on new critical cluster_id combinations.

Severity routing: any plugin_register_failures_1h greater than zero after joining cluster_id or vcenter_fqdn is critical for the vCenter bridge plus HCI owner_team. SSO under eight days critical as written. Missed scheduled jobs beyond lookup target routes high. api_p99_latency_ms above five thousand or hourly p99 drift above one thousand milliseconds routes warning. overall_health warning routes warning. change_freeze_until in the future forces severity_bucket info so CAB windows stay quiet while a parallel reporting search without the freeze clause keeps the audit trail.

Provide a duplicate saved search without the final where to populate a twenty four hour management health dashboard. The fenced SPL at the end of this field duplicates the spl property for workbook paste.

Step 3 — Validate

Use vSphere Client navigation Menu then Administration then Solutions then Client Plugins to read the Dell VxRail plugin state and release string, then compare the newest initialize related field in vxrail:vxm:system to the same clock hour. Open Hosts and Clusters, pick the cluster, Configure, VxRail, and confirm the HTML5 summary loads; if the UI spinner never clears while REST is two hundred OK, treat vmware:vc:event as authoritative for admin impact.

Sign in directly to the VxRail Manager web appliance with a human admin separate from automation and confirm dashboards render; mirror one GET in curl with the service account for parity with Splunk.

Where Dell permits shell on the VxRail Manager guest, run mystic-cli system status and mystic-cli sso status, paste stdout into the change record, and reconcile field by field to HEC JSON after your normalizers. In a sandbox only, deliberately set an incorrect SSO service password in vCenter to watch vsphere_sso_state flip and confirm Splunk matches the UI before you restore the correct password from the vault.

Keep reminding reviewers that UC-19.2.13 may remain green while this UC fires because UC-19.2.13 is cluster data plane summary, not HTML5 registration. UC-19.2.26 may still show Idle LCM while schedulers slip; UC-19.2.37 can show comfortable free space while SSO trust is broken; silencing here because storage looks fine is the wrong outcome.

Step 4 — Operationalize

Row one single value of VxM count with severity critical. Row two single value of minimum sso_days_remaining. Row three table of scheduled jobs with overdue highlighting. Row four timechart of api_p99_latency_ms with reference line from lookup. Drilldown panels for raw vmware:vc:log lines containing ExtensionManager during incident hours.

Runbook sequence: verify via vCenter UI, capture Splunk row, attempt mystic-cli recommended re-register, refresh SSO credentials per Dell article for your major version, supervised restart of Tomcat only when Dell supports it, and open Dell support with svmsupport bundle correlation plus Splunk excerpts when two cycles fail. Assign ownership to HCI platform with identity partnership for SSO. Tie operations reviews to UC-19.2.13 for host health, UC-19.2.26 before LCM execution, UC-19.2.37 before host evacuation maintenance.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting

VxRail Manager platform upgrade legitimately replays extension registrations; expect bursts in vmware:vc:event; mark change_freeze_until on the platform change and page only when failures persist past the vendor documented stabilization window.

vCenter Server upgrade or patch uninstalls and reinstalls client extensions; compare both VxM appliances on stretched clusters independently and align timestamps with VMware maintenance records.

Supervised Tomcat restart for log rotation can create sixty to one hundred eighty seconds of REST timeout; confirm vxrail:vxm:probe recovery before treating as sustained outage.

Planned quarterly SSO password rotation for the mystic user yields temporary trust mismatch until operators update the secret in VxRail; align paging with the rotation ticket so the warning is expected.

Cross vCenter migration demands old extension cleanup and new registration; failures often reflect stale extension keys until administrators complete Dell documented migration tasks, not random Splunk drift.

Enterprise CA or VxM appliance certificate replacement rotates fingerprints without implying outage; compare openssl s_client output to CMDB expectations instead of auto rolling back a signed rotation.

HCI Mesh discovery scans may raise short lived lcm component warnings; correlate narrow spikes with Dell noted discovery windows before you rebuild trust.

Fenced SPL duplicate for alerting workbook matches the spl property verbatim inside the following fence.

Meta reminders: rotate HEC token quarterly, keep lookup ownership with phone numbers current, document stretched requirement that both VxM rows must pass before closing sev one on a stretched resource.

Governance paragraph for audit: evidence is the saved search output plus vCenter Client Plugins screenshot and optional mystic-cli transcript in the ticket; retention thirteen months online mirrors sibling VxRail UCs.

Field evolution: when Dell renames mystic_node_state, update poller mapping and bump poll_schema_version inside JSON so silent field loss is obvious in fieldsummary queries.

Capacity and performance: each poll set is small JSON; the heavy volume is vmware event streams—trim at props to extension related types when necessary.

Ownership: Head of IT Operations remains accountable in RACI, delegate execution to named owner_team per row.

Stretched clusters carry two vxm_fqdn rows in the lookup; alert logic treats them independently so one dead manager is visible even if the second is fine.

Self Deployment Mode clusters or satellite designs may lack standard VxRail Manager DNS names; exclude them via exclusions rather than forcing bad joins.

Security: restrict who can read SSO certificate fields at search time because the events contain infrastructure identity metadata.

Drill link pattern: pass tokenized vxm_fqdn into a secondary search for raw vxrail:vxm:scheduled_jobs across seven days to see historical miss streaks.

Synthetic probe false positive: WAN jitter through a jump host can raise p99 without VxM fault; baseline per site before hard thresholds.

Operational review cadence monthly for the first quarter after go live, quarterly thereafter unless a major VxRail train changes API surface.

Documentation hygiene: store poller Git URL and Splunk app version beside the lookup so auditors can trace configuration drift.

Escalation matrix: severity critical to primary HCI plus identity if SSO critical; high to HCI duty manager; warning to business hours team unless paired with customer facing change window.

Integration with ticketing: include vxm_fqdn, cluster_id, severity_bucket, plugin_register_failures_1h, sso_days_remaining, missed_jobs_24h, api_p99_latency_ms in the work note body for one click Splunk replay.

Testing harness: maintain a json fixtures directory with sample HEC lines per sourcetype reviewed by a second engineer before production promotion.

Risk acceptance: if a site refuses vmware:vc:event collection, document the residual blind spot where REST healthy but UI dead cannot alarm on extension failure counts.

Long term: evaluate moving OAuth to short lived tokens with automatic rotation hooks tied to the vault to reduce manual password drama.

Closing: when Splunk Cloud limits join fanout, materialize five minute summaries into summary index si_vxm_mgmt using collect or recurring scheduled searches, then point this alert at the summary index with the same column names to keep search cost predictable.

Plain language summary for executives sits in value and grandma fields; keep engineering depth here for implementers without marketing adjectives.

Reminder duplicate: SSO cert threshold warn at thirty days in narrative aligns to target_min_sso_days_remaining default; tune per enterprise PKI lead if internal policy demands ninety.

Synthetic duplication note: two independent arms gather plugin failures by cluster_id and by vcenter_fqdn so incomplete TA field sets still score non zero counts.

Stretch goal: add optional ninth column in lookup for witness_only boolean to suppress certain warnings on secondary VxM if Dell support instructs.

Final duplicate caution: never paste credentials into comments or into Splunk searches; keep vault references only.

End of detailed implementation narrative for UC nineteen dot two dot thirty two management plane focus.

```spl
`comment("UC-19.2.32 VxRail management plane: vCenter extension failure corroboration, VxM SSO trust horizon, internal scheduled health jobs, TLS API heartbeat p99. SOURCES: vxrail:vxm:system, vxrail:vxm:sso, vxrail:vxm:scheduled_jobs, vxrail:vxm:health, vxrail:vxm:probe, vmware:vc:event, vmware:vc:log. LOOKUP: vxrail_vxm_owner (vxm_fqdn, cluster_id, owner_team, target_min_sso_days_remaining, target_max_api_p99_ms, target_max_missed_jobs_24h, change_freeze_until; optional vcenter_fqdn). OWNER: hci_platform. FREEZE: change_freeze_until demotes routed rows to info.")`
| inputlookup vxrail_vxm_owner
| eval _time=now()
| eval vxm_fqdn=tostring(coalesce(vxm_fqdn, vxmHost, manager_fqdn, ""))
| eval cluster_id=tostring(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, ""))
| eval owner_team=tostring(coalesce(owner_team, ownerTeam, ""))
| eval target_min_sso_days_remaining=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_min_sso_days_remaining, targetMinSsoDaysRemaining, "30")), 10)
| eval target_max_api_p99_ms=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_api_p99_ms, targetMaxApiP99Ms, "5000")), 10)
| eval target_max_missed_jobs_24h=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(target_max_missed_jobs_24h, targetMaxMissedJobs24h, "1")), 10)
| eval change_freeze_until=tostring(coalesce(change_freeze_until, changeFreezeUntil, ""))
| eval vcenter_fqdn=tostring(coalesce(vcenter_fqdn, vcenterFqdn, ""))
| join type=left max=0 cluster_id
    [| search (index=vmware OR index=vxrail OR index=hci) (sourcetype="vmware:vc:event" OR sourcetype="vmware:vc:log") earliest=-1h@h latest=@h
      | eval cid=tostring(coalesce(cluster_id, clusterId, computeResourceId, compute_resource_id, ""))
      | eval evt=tostring(coalesce(EventTypeId, event_type, type, Type, ""))
      | eval raw_l=tostring(_raw)
      | eval is_ext_fail=if(((like(evt, "%RegisterExtensionFailedEvent%")) OR (like(evt, "%PluginUnpackFailedEvent%")) OR (match(raw_l, "(?i)RegisterExtensionFailed")) OR (match(raw_l, "(?i)PluginUnpackFailed"))), 1, 0)
      | where ((is_ext_fail=1) AND (len(cid)>0))
      | stats count AS plugin_register_cluster BY cid
      | rename cid AS cluster_id ]
| join type=left max=0 vcenter_fqdn
    [| search (index=vmware OR index=vxrail OR index=hci) (sourcetype="vmware:vc:event" OR sourcetype="vmware:vc:log") earliest=-1h@h latest=@h
      | eval vckey=tostring(coalesce(vcenter, vCenter, vcenter_fqdn, host, ""))
      | eval evt=tostring(coalesce(EventTypeId, event_type, type, Type, ""))
      | eval raw_l=tostring(_raw)
      | eval is_ext_fail=if(((like(evt, "%RegisterExtensionFailedEvent%")) OR (like(evt, "%PluginUnpackFailedEvent%")) OR (match(raw_l, "(?i)RegisterExtensionFailed")) OR (match(raw_l, "(?i)PluginUnpackFailed"))), 1, 0)
      | where ((is_ext_fail=1) AND (len(vckey)>0))
      | stats count AS plugin_register_vc BY vckey
      | rename vckey AS vcenter_fqdn ]
| eval plugin_register_failures_1h=max(coalesce(plugin_register_cluster, 0), coalesce(plugin_register_vc, 0))
| join type=left max=0 vxm_fqdn
    [| search index=vxrail sourcetype="vxrail:vxm:sso" earliest=-24h@h latest=@h
      | eval vxm_fqdn=tostring(coalesce(vxm_fqdn, vxmHost, manager_fqdn, ""))
      | eval exp_raw=tostring(coalesce(vsphere_sso_certificate_expiry, vsphereSsoCertificateExpiry, ""))
      | eval exp_epoch=coalesce(strptime(exp_raw, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), strptime(exp_raw, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z"), strptime(exp_raw, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"), strptime(exp_raw, "%Y-%m-%d"), 0)
      | eval sso_days_remaining=if((exp_epoch>0), ceil((exp_epoch - now()) / 86400), null())
      | eval sso_trust_state=tostring(coalesce(vsphere_sso_state, vsphereSsoState, ""))
      | stats latest(sso_days_remaining) AS sso_days_remaining latest(sso_trust_state) AS sso_trust_state max(_time) AS sso_poll_time BY vxm_fqdn ]
| join type=left max=0 vxm_fqdn
    [| search index=vxrail sourcetype="vxrail:vxm:scheduled_jobs" earliest=-24h@h latest=@h
      | eval vxm_fqdn=tostring(coalesce(vxm_fqdn, vxmHost, manager_fqdn, ""))
      | eval last_run_epoch=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(last_run_epoch, lastRunEpoch, "0")), 10)
      | eval next_run_epoch=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(next_run_epoch, nextRunEpoch, "0")), 10)
      | eval last_status=tostring(coalesce(last_status, lastStatus, ""))
      | eval now_wall=now()
      | eval grace=900
      | eval missed=if((((next_run_epoch>0) AND (now_wall>(next_run_epoch+grace))) AND ((last_run_epoch<next_run_epoch) OR match(last_status, "(?i)fail|error|timeout"))), 1, 0)
      | stats sum(missed) AS missed_jobs_24h BY vxm_fqdn ]
| join type=left max=0 vxm_fqdn
    [| search index=vxrail (sourcetype="vxrail:vxm:probe" OR sourcetype="vxrail:vxm:system") earliest=-24h@h latest=@h
      | eval vxm_fqdn=tostring(coalesce(vxm_fqdn, vxmHost, manager_fqdn, ""))
      | eval latency_ms=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(http_latency_ms, httpLatencyMs, total_time_ms, curl_time_ms, poll_latency_ms, "0")), 10)
      | where ((latency_ms>0) AND (len(vxm_fqdn)>0))
      | bin _time span=1h
      | stats perc99(latency_ms) AS hourly_p99 BY vxm_fqdn, _time
      | sort 0 + vxm_fqdn + _time
      | streamstats current=f window=24 last(hourly_p99) AS p99_now first(hourly_p99) AS p99_start BY vxm_fqdn
      | eval api_p99_trend_delta=round(p99_now - p99_start, 2)
      | stats latest(hourly_p99) AS api_p99_latency_ms latest(api_p99_trend_delta) AS api_p99_trend_delta BY vxm_fqdn ]
| join type=left max=0 vxm_fqdn
    [| search index=vxrail sourcetype="vxrail:vxm:health" earliest=-2h@h latest=@h
      | eval vxm_fqdn=tostring(coalesce(vxm_fqdn, vxmHost, manager_fqdn, ""))
      | eval vxm_health_state=tostring(coalesce(overall_health, overallHealth, health, ""))
      | stats latest(vxm_health_state) AS vxm_health_state BY vxm_fqdn ]
| eval api_p99_latency_ms=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(api_p99_latency_ms, "0")), 10)
| eventstats median(sso_days_remaining) AS org_median_sso_days BY owner_team
| eventstats min(sso_days_remaining) AS org_min_sso_fleet
| eval sso_delta_vs_org=if((isnotnull(sso_days_remaining) AND isnotnull(org_median_sso_days)), round(sso_days_remaining - org_median_sso_days, 1), null())
| eval sso_vs_fleet_min=if((isnotnull(sso_days_remaining) AND isnotnull(org_min_sso_fleet)), round(sso_days_remaining - org_min_sso_fleet, 1), null())
| eval cft1=strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z")
| eval cft2=strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| eval cft3=strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")
| eval cft4=strptime(change_freeze_until, "%Y-%m-%d")
| eval freeze_active=if((len(trim(change_freeze_until))>0) AND (now()<coalesce(cft1, cft2, cft3, cft4, 0)), 1, 0)
| eval sev_raw=case(
    ((plugin_register_failures_1h>0)), "critical",
    ((isnotnull(sso_days_remaining)) AND ((sso_days_remaining<=7) OR (sso_days_remaining<1) OR match(sso_trust_state, "(?i)invalid|fail|error|expired"))), "critical",
    ((missed_jobs_24h>target_max_missed_jobs_24h)), "high",
    ((isnotnull(sso_days_remaining)) AND (sso_days_remaining<=target_min_sso_days_remaining)), "high",
    (((api_p99_latency_ms+0)>target_max_api_p99_ms) OR ((api_p99_trend_delta+0)>1000)), "warning",
    ((match(vxm_health_state, "(?i)warning|error|degraded")) OR ((isnotnull(api_p99_trend_delta)) AND ((api_p99_trend_delta+0)>500))), "warning",
    1=1, "info" )
| eval severity_bucket=if((freeze_active=1), "info", sev_raw)
| where severity_bucket!="info"
| table vxm_fqdn, cluster_id, owner_team, severity_bucket, sev_raw, freeze_active, plugin_register_failures_1h, sso_days_remaining, sso_trust_state, org_median_sso_days, org_min_sso_fleet, sso_delta_vs_org, sso_vs_fleet_min, missed_jobs_24h, api_p99_latency_ms, api_p99_trend_delta, vxm_health_state, target_max_api_p99_ms, target_min_sso_days_remaining, target_max_missed_jobs_24h
```
grandmaExplanation: We watch the admin screens that connect VMware vCenter to the VxRail control service, the login trust that keeps that connection honest, and the timers that run background health checks. We raise a clear signal when those control pieces break so repairs happen before someone walks into a change window with a frozen console.
hardware: Dell VxRail E-Series V-Series P-Series S-Series D-Series HCI appliances; VxRail E660 V670 P670 S670 class nodes on fourteenth generation PowerEdge; VxRail E660N V670N P670N fifteenth generation PowerEdge based builds; VxRail PE built nodes on PowerEdge R650 R750 class hosts where Dell ships them inside the VxRail product envelope; VxRail HCI Mesh designs with satellite storage relationships; VxRail Dynamic Nodes compute only footprints where HTML5 plugin scope may vary by management attach; VxRail Stretched Cluster layouts with two VxRail Manager instances that both require healthy SSO scheduler and plugin ties to the paired vCenter.
